Title: Altering carbon allocation in hybrid poplar (Populus alba × grandidentata) impacts cell wall growth and development

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Raffinose family oligosaccharide (RFO) biosynthesis; myo-inositol/galactinol metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Galactinol synthesis step supplying RFO biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactosyl from UDP-galactose to myo-inositol to form galactinol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and galactosyl donor for raffinose biosynthesis; elevates galactinol that acts as a signaling metabolite affecting carbon allocation and cell wall deposition
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Presented as a pivotal enzyme in RFO synthesis; key control step for galactinol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene detected in phloem, developing xylem, and source leaves; highest in lines 6 and 11 in phloem and developing xylem
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive overexpression in greenhouse-grown plants under supplemental light
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transport/storage-associated soluble carbohydrate precursor; implicated in abiotic/biotic stress protection and signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in poplar; RT-PCR; soluble sugar HPLC profiling; starch assay and Lugol staining; structural carbohydrate and lignin analysis; histochemistry; immunolabeling; 2D NMR; X-ray diffraction; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases galactinol, raffinose, and xylem/phloem starch; shifts carbon allocation; increases cellulose and crystallinity; decreases lignin and mannose/xylose; induces tension-wood-like xylem and severe stunting in high-expression lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of Arabidopsis AtGolS3 in hybrid poplar strongly increased galactinol in phloem, developing xylem, and leaves, with raffinose appearing in xylem of transgenic lines. 2. High-AtGolS3 lines showed reduced sucrose in sink tissues, elevated xylem starch, and transcriptomic up-regulation of myo-inositol synthesis and sucrose degradation genes, indicating altered carbon partitioning. 3. These metabolic changes were associated with increased cellulose and crystallinity, reduced lignin, altered hemicellulose composition, G-layer formation, and a tension-wood-like phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Extreme overexpression caused severe stunting, reduced stem diameter, and altered architecture/vineline phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to engineer higher cellulose/lower lignin biomass for bioenergy; expression should be moderated or driven by a xylem-specific promoter to avoid growth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes that GolS overexpression and/or elevated galactinol likely functions as a molecular signal initiating broader metabolic and developmental responses rather than only changing RFO flux
